questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The committee was asked to __________ the report before submitting it to the management.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate answer is: b Scrutinize - To scrutinize means to examine or inspect closely and thoroughly. This option fits well because it indicates that the committee should carefully check the report for any errors or issues before submission. Summarize - To summarize means to provide a brief statement of the main points. While summarizing might be part of the review process, it doesn't imply a thorough examination of the report. Compile - To compile means to collect and assemble information into a report or document. This action is usually done before the report is reviewed, so it doesn't fit well in the context of what the committee is asked to do before submission. Neglect - To neglect means to fail to care for or pay proper attention to something. This is the opposite of what is expected in the context of preparing a report for submission.

questions.question: Select the correct direct form of the given sentence. The coach asked the student player why he had been absent the previous day.
options: The coach said to the player, "Why you absent yesterday?"
The coach said to the player, "Why are you absent yesterday?"
The coach said to the player, "Why were you absent yesterday?"
The coach said to the player, "Why you were absent yesterday?"
questions.explanation: The most appropriate answer is c: The coach said to the player, "Why were you absent yesterday?" Direct speech ---- Indirect speech Simple past tense ---- Past perfect tense

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. Anita’s grandmother presented me a cute, brown, small puppy on my fifteenth birthday and I was filled with joy because that was my first puppy.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate answer is c: Cute, small, brown puppy Adjectives usually follow a particular order when more than one is used to describe a noun. The common order is: Quantity or number < Quality or opinion < Size < Age < Shape < Color Proper adjective (often nationality, other place of origin, or material) Purpose or qualifier Applying this order to the adjectives in the given sentence ("cute, brown, small puppy"), Therefore, the most appropriate option is: Cute, small, brown puppy

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word in the following sentence. They should realise that the only way to meet these objectives is to strike a negotiated deal, rather than proloneging the war that has destroyed much of Gaza and turned Israel into an internationally isolated rogue state.
questions.explanation: The incorrectly spelt word in the given sentence is: Proloneging The correct spelling is "prolonging." Negotiated: Discussed and reached an agreement through formal discussions. Prolonging: Extending the duration of something. Destroyed: Caused something to no longer exist or be usable. Isolated: Detached or separated from others.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The team was discussing the project details.
options: The project detail was being discussed by the team.
The project details were being discussing by the team.
The project details were discussed by the team.
The project details were being discussed by the team.
questions.explanation: The correct option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice is: The project details were being discussed by the team. Active voice ---- Passive voice Subject + was/were + V(ing) +Object ---- Object + was/were + being + V3 + by + Subject Therefore, the correct answer is: “The project details were being discussed by the team.”

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Making a loud and confused noise.
questions.explanation: The one-word substitute for "making a loud and confused noise" is: Clamorous Clamorous means making a loud and confused noise, often associated with shouting or uproar. Argue and squabble involve verbal disagreements but do not necessarily imply loud or confused noise. Bicker refers to arguing in a petty or trivial manner. Hence, Clamorous best fits the description provided.

questions.question: Select the correct synonym of the given word. Wax
questions.explanation: The correct synonym of the word "wax" is: Enlarge Wax: To increase in size, volume, or intensity. Enlarge: To make something bigger; to increase in size, extent, or scope. Shrill: Having or emitting a sharp, high-pitched sound. Shrink: To decrease in size, range, or extent; to become smaller. Wane: To decrease in size, extent, or degree; to diminish or decline.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Abduct
questions.explanation: The most appropriate antonym of the word "abduct" is: Redeem Abduct: To take someone away illegally by force or deception, typically to obtain ransom or for political reasons. Redeem: To compensate for the faults or bad aspects of something; to make up for or set free. Kidnap: To take someone away illegally by force, typically in order to obtain a ransom. Detain: To keep someone in official custody, typically for questioning about a crime or in a situation where they are not free to leave. Apprehend: To arrest someone for a crime.

questions.question: Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains a grammatical error. Not only the students/ but also the teacher/ were excited about/ the upcoming field trip.
questions.explanation: The part of the sentence that contains a grammatical error is: were excited about The correct verb form should be singular to agree with the singular subject "teacher." This correction ensures that the verb "was" agrees with the singular subject "teacher." Therefore, the correct option should be: “Not only the students but also the teacher was excited about the upcoming field trip.”

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom that can be substituted in the following sentence. She felt fortunate to have a bite at the cherry when a new job opening came up at her dream company.
options: A chance to try something new and exciting.
An unexpected opportunity to achieve success or reach a desired outcome.
A repeated attempt to accomplish something that was not successful the first time.
A second chance or opportunity to try again after a previous attempt failed.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate meaning of the idiom "a bite at the cherry" in the context of the sentence is: An unexpected opportunity to achieve success or reach a desired outcome. This meaning aligns with the situation where she felt fortunate to have the chance to apply for a new job opening at her dream company, which was an unexpected opportunity that came her way.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. I hadn't visited my hometown since I moved to the city.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence is: haven't visited If 'since' is followed by 'simple past tense', 'since' is preceded by 'present perfect tense'. Therefore, the corrected sentence would be: “I haven’t visited my hometown since I moved to the city.”

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. The manager's decision to use an anodyne email response avoided escalating the conflict with the client.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate synonym of the underlined word "anodyne" in the given sentence is: Palliative Anodyne: not likely to cause offence or disagreement and somewhat dull. Palliative: Means soothing or relieving without curing, which aligns well with the meaning of "anodyne" in the sentence provided. Unattractive: Means not pleasing or appealing visually, which is not related to the context of calming or soothing. Agitating: Means causing anxiety or irritation, the opposite of what "anodyne" implies. Vexing: Means causing irritation or distress, also not synonymous with soothing or calming.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to complete the given sentence. Despite being fresh out of college, she landed _______________ at a prestigious law firm.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to complete the sentence is: A plum job A plum job: A highly desirable or attractive job, typically offering good pay, prestige, and opportunities for advancement. A ray of hope: A small sign of hope or optimism in a difficult situation. Dead end job: A job with no opportunities for advancement or career growth. Bang up job: To do an excellent or impressive job.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the given sentence. The detective beseeched the witnesses to come forward with any information they had about the crime.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate antonym of the underlined word "beseeched" in the given sentence is: Spurn Beseech: To ask someone urgently and fervently to do something; to implore or entreat. Spurn: To reject with disdain or contempt; to refuse or decline something scornfully. Solicit: To ask for or try to obtain something from someone, typically in a formal or persistent manner. Plea: A sincere or earnest request; an appeal or entreaty. Implore: To beg someone earnestly or desperately to do something.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. After a long and arduous battle, the exhausted fighters _________________ to indicate they could no longer continue.
options: Keep the wolf from the door
questions.explanation: The most appropriate answer is: showed the white flag Showed the white flag: To surrender or admit defeat. It comes from the practice of waving a white flag to indicate surrender in battle. Move heaven and earth: To do everything possible to achieve something. It means making a great effort or taking extreme measures to accomplish a goal. Keep the wolf from the door: To have just enough money to avoid going hungry or to prevent financial ruin. It means managing to survive financially. Blow his own trumpet: To boast or brag about one's own achievements or abilities.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. The baby just learned to crawl, and this is moving around the room quickly.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment "this is" in the given sentence is "it is." It is: This pronoun "it" is used for things, non-living objects, and infants when the gender is not specified. Correct: "It" is appropriate because the baby's gender is not mentioned. It is standard to refer to infants as "it" when the gender is not known or specified. Therefore, the corrected sentence would be: "The baby just learned to crawl, and it is moving around the room quickly."

questions.question: Select the sentence with the appropriate use of preposition.
options: The decision was made after consulting amid the committee members.
The decision was made after consulting beside the committee members.
The decision was made after consulting among the committee members.
The decision was made after consulting between the committee members.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is c: “The decision was made after consulting among the committee members.” "Among" is the correct preposition to use here as it indicates interactions or consultations within a group of more than two members. It is the most appropriate and commonly used term in modern English.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them contains an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. The mayor along with the city council are responsible for urban planning decisions.
questions.explanation: The part that contains the error is: the city council are According to the rule, when two subjects are joined by "along with," "as well as," "together with," etc., the verb should agree with the first subject ("the mayor" in this case). Therefore, the correct sentence should be: "The mayor along with the city council is responsible for urban planning decisions."

questions.question: Rearrange the parts of the sentence in correct order. In addition to P. Many activities that support agriculture and village life are also sources of livelihood for people in rural India. Q. Agriculture is the single most important source of livelihood for the majority of the rural population. But the rural is not just agriculture. R. For example, a large number of artisans such as potters, carpenters, weavers, ironsmiths, and goldsmiths are found in rural areas. S. They were once part and parcel of the village economy. Their numbers have been steadily lessening since the colonial period.
questions.explanation: The correct order of the parts of the sentence is: QPRS So, the complete rearranged sentence would be: "Q. Agriculture is the single most important source of livelihood for the majority of the rural population. But the rural is not just agriculture. P. Many activities that support agriculture and village life are also sources of livelihood for people in rural India. R. For example, a large number of artisans such as potters, carpenters, weavers, ironsmiths, and goldsmiths are found in rural areas. S. They were once part and parcel of the village economy. Their numbers have been steadily lessening since the colonial period."

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word in the following sentence. The “slight regression,” according to the report, is mainly due to “small declines” in the spheres of education and political empowerment. With a population of over 140 crore, even two steps back mean stagering numbers.
questions.explanation: The incorrectly spelled word in the sentence is: Stagering The correct spelling should be: Staggering Staggering: Extremely impressive or surprising; causing great astonishment or disbelief due to its size, scale, or intensity. Regression: The act or process of moving backward; in the context of the sentence, it refers to a slight decline or setback. Empowerment: The process of giving someone the authority or power to do something; often used in the context of giving individuals or groups the ability to take control of their own lives or circumstances. Population: The total number of people living in a particular area, country, or region at a specific time.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The history of colonisation reveals that rich resources in colonies were the main (1) __________ for the foreign invaders. It was primarily the higher level of technological development of the colonising countries that helped them to (2) __________ resources of other regions and establish their supremacy over the colonies. Therefore, resources can contribute to development only when they are (3) ___________ by appropriate technological development and institutional changes. India (4) __________ all this in different phases of colonisation. Therefore, in India, development, in general, and resource development in particular does not only involve the availability of resources, (5) __________ the technology, quality of human resources and the historical experiences of the people. Select the most appropriate answer to fill in blank 1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer to fill in blank number 1 is: Attractions This sentence discusses the primary reason why foreign invaders were interested in colonies. We need a word that accurately reflects the economic or strategic motivation for their actions. points: Typically refers to particular positions or locations, not suitable in this context. attractions: Implies something that draws interest or allure, but may not fully convey the economic or strategic intent of invaders. scenery: Refers to the natural landscape or surroundings, which is not relevant to the context of colonization. views: Refers to opinions or perspectives, which doesn't fit the context of colonization. Given the context that rich resources were the main motivation for foreign invaders to colonize, "attractions" fits best as it suggests something that was sought after or desired.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The history of colonisation reveals that rich resources in colonies were the main (1) __________ for the foreign invaders. It was primarily the higher level of technological development of the colonising countries that helped them to (2) __________ resources of other regions and establish their supremacy over the colonies. Therefore, resources can contribute to development only when they are (3) ___________ by appropriate technological development and institutional changes. India (4) __________ all this in different phases of colonisation. Therefore, in India, development, in general, and resource development in particular does not only involve the availability of resources, (5) __________ the technology, quality of human resources and the historical experiences of the people. Select the most appropriate answer to fill in blank 2.
questions.explanation: The correct answer to fill in blank number 2 is: Exploit This sentence discusses how technological development aided colonizers in dealing with resources in other regions. We need a word that aligns with the idea of utilizing or making the most of these resources. Ignore: Means to disregard or pay no attention to, opposite in meaning to making use of resources. Abandon: Means to give up or leave behind, which does not fit the context of using resources. Exploit: Means to make full use of and derive benefit from, which directly relates to using resources effectively. Preserve: Means to protect and maintain, which is the opposite of using resources for gain. Given the context that higher technological development helped colonizers in utilizing resources, the correct answer is: Exploit

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The history of colonisation reveals that rich resources in colonies were the main (1) __________ for the foreign invaders. It was primarily the higher level of technological development of the colonising countries that helped them to (2) __________ resources of other regions and establish their supremacy over the colonies. Therefore, resources can contribute to development only when they are (3) ___________ by appropriate technological development and institutional changes. India (4) __________ all this in different phases of colonisation. Therefore, in India, development, in general, and resource development in particular does not only involve the availability of resources, (5) __________ the technology, quality of human resources and the historical experiences of the people. Select the most appropriate answer to fill in blank 3.
questions.explanation: The correct answer to fill in blank number 3 is: accompanied This sentence discusses how technological development aided colonizers in dealing with resources in other regions. We need a word that aligns with the idea of utilizing or making the most of these resources. Ignore: Means to disregard or pay no attention to, opposite in meaning to making use of resources. Abandon: Means to give up or leave behind, which does not fit the context of using resources. Exploit: Means to make full use of and derive benefit from, which directly relates to using resources effectively. Preserve: Means to protect and maintain, which is the opposite of using resources for gain.

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The history of colonisation reveals that rich resources in colonies were the main (1) __________ for the foreign invaders. It was primarily the higher level of technological development of the colonising countries that helped them to (2) __________ resources of other regions and establish their supremacy over the colonies. Therefore, resources can contribute to development only when they are (3) ___________ by appropriate technological development and institutional changes. India (4) __________ all this in different phases of colonisation. Therefore, in India, development, in general, and resource development in particular does not only involve the availability of resources, (5) __________ the technology, quality of human resources and the historical experiences of the people. Select the most appropriate answer to fill in blank 4.
questions.explanation: The correct answer to fill in blank number 4 is: has experienced has experienced: This is correct and appropriate for the singular subject "India" in present perfect tense, indicating that India has gone through these phases up to the present. have experienced: This is incorrect because "have" is used with plural subjects, and "India" is singular. was experienced: This is incorrect because it suggests that India was the subject of the experience in a passive voice, which doesn't fit the context. were experienced: This is incorrect because "were" is used with plural subjects, and "India" is singular. Given the context, the correct answer is: has experienced Therefore, the sentence would read: "India has experienced all this in different phases of colonisation."

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The history of colonisation reveals that rich resources in colonies were the main (1) __________ for the foreign invaders. It was primarily the higher level of technological development of the colonising countries that helped them to (2) __________ resources of other regions and establish their supremacy over the colonies. Therefore, resources can contribute to development only when they are (3) ___________ by appropriate technological development and institutional changes. India (4) __________ all this in different phases of colonisation. Therefore, in India, development, in general, and resource development in particular does not only involve the availability of resources, (5) __________ the technology, quality of human resources and the historical experiences of the people. Select the most appropriate answer to fill in blank 5.
questions.explanation: The correct answer to fill in blank number 5 is: but also so that: Indicates purpose, not appropriate for listing additional factors. other than: Implies exclusion or contrast, which doesn't fit the context of inclusion. but also: Completes the idea of "not only... but also," correctly indicating the inclusion of additional factors. whereas: Indicates contrast, not appropriate for this context of inclusion. Given the context, the correct answer is: but also
